name: Focus Integrity Ontology

thesis_layers.L1: Anatomy — biological structures that make optical focus possible
thesis_layers.L2: Perception — cognitive systems that convert visual input into clarity
thesis_layers.L3: Machine Vision & Verification — artificial systems that detect, interpret, and verify

entry_criteria: Failure or condition of focus, not of vision in general
Maps to at least one thesis layer (L1, L2, or L3)
Source-backed or explicitly marked as conceptual interpretation
Connects to at least two existing reference units

classes.name: Suspension Failure
classes.definition: The holding structures that keep the focal element in position themselves fail.
classes.governing_claim: When the suspension layer fails, the focal element is present but unstable.

layers.L1: Zonular weakness, dislocation, or rupture compromising lens position.
layers.L2: Collapse of attentional anchoring; focal target cannot be held against competing stimuli.
layers.L3: Loss of calibration or spatial grounding; the model's reference frame degrades.

classes.name: Accommodation Failure
classes.definition: The system cannot adjust focus across its intended operating range.
classes.governing_claim: A system that focuses at one setting only does not have focus integrity.

layers.L2: Failure of perceptual constancy as conditions change.
layers.L3: Domain shift; correct focus in training conditions, failure in new ones.

classes.name: Signal–Noise Failure
classes.definition: The system cannot separate meaningful signal from noise.
classes.governing_claim: Focus without separation produces sharp noise, not meaning.

layers.L1: Optical scatter or media opacity reducing contrast at the receptor.
layers.L3: Adversarial perturbation, synthetic noise, or low signal-to-noise input.

classes.name: Provenance Failure
classes.definition: The system cannot establish whether what it sees is real or where it came from.
classes.governing_claim: In an age of synthetic images, focus without provenance cannot yield visual truth.

layers.L1: Conceptual bridge only — not strongly applicable at the anatomical layer.
layers.L2: Misattributed source of a percept; false confidence in an illusion.
layers.L3: Deepfake and synthetic media; absent or broken image provenance.

classes.name: Interpretation Failure
classes.definition: Focus is held and signal is clean, but the resulting meaning is wrong.
classes.governing_claim: The final failure of focus is not blur — it is confident misreading.

layers.L1: Conceptual bridge only — interpretation is not an anatomical property.
layers.L2: Perceptual error; correct sharp percept, incorrect meaning.
layers.L3: Machine sight without machine understanding; correct detection, wrong interpretation.
